action.reasoning: Observation: 10 epochs CLIP-B/16 plateaued around val_macro_f1=0.091 (eval=0.064). Training F1 isn't improving past epoch 7. Analysis: The model is plateauing — likely need (1) higher resolution or (2) larger model. With 5h left, ~6min/epoch for B/16 at 224. CLIP-L/14 was ~40min/epoch at 224. Better strategy: continue B/16 longer with class-balanced strategy or switch to higher resolution. Actually the eval F1 (0.064) is much lower than val (0.091) — there's a domain gap. Key issue: long-tailed classes need more representation. Let me try (1) higher resolution 336 with CLIP-B/16, OR (2) use a logit-adjusted loss to handle long-tail. Actually let me try training CLIP-L/14 at 224 with better hyperparameters — given time constraints, 4 epochs at ~40min = 2.7h. With 5h left that's feasible. Decision: Train CLIP-L/14 with cosine schedule, mild logit adjustment for long-tail, 3-4 epochs. First, let me modify train.py to add logit-adjusted loss and switch to L/14.
